perturbation.original_text: The Company agrees that its use of the Trademarks and the Good Sam name and logo is restricted to the Approved Use, and the Company shall not use the Trademarks or the Good Sam name or logo for any other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company generally agrees that its use of the Trademarks and the Good Sam name and logo should primarily be for the Approved Use, and the Company will attempt not to use the Trademarks or the Good Sam name or logo for other purposes, as much as possible.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces ambiguity and weakens the restriction on the use of the Trademarks. Replacing 'is restricted to' with 'should primarily be for' and 'shall not use' with 'will attempt not to use, as much as possible' makes the obligation non-binding and allows for discretionary use outside the 'Approved Use'.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Business and Professions Code Section 14220
perturbation.law_explanation: California Business and Professions Code Section 14220 defines trademark infringement, which occurs when someone uses a trademark without the owner's consent in a way that is likely to cause confusion. By weakening the restriction on the Company's use of the trademark, the modified text increases the risk of trademark infringement, which is contradictory to ensuring the use of the mark is restricted to "Approved Use" as trademarks require.

BUSINESS RIGHTS [14000 - 14704] ( Division 6 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 56. ) CHAPTER 2. Model State Trademark Law [14200 - 14272] ( Chapter 2 repealed and added by Stats. 2007, Ch. 711, Sec. 2. ) ARTICLE 4. Assignments, Changes of Name, and Other Instruments [14220- 14220.] ( Article 4 added by Stats. 2007, Ch. 711, Sec. 2. ) 14220. (a) Any mark and its registration hereunder shall be assignable with the good will of the business in which the mark is used, or with that part of the good will of the business connected with the use of and symbolized by the mark. Assignment shall be by instrument in writing duly executed and may be recorded with the secretary upon the payment of the recording fee payable to the secretary as set forth in subdivision (b) of Section 12193 of the Government Code, who, upon recording of the assignment, shall issue in the name of the assignee a new certificate for the remainder of the term of the registration or of the last renewal thereof. An assignment of any registration under this chapter shall be void as against any subsequent purchaser for valuable consideration without notice, unless it is recorded with the secretary within three months after the date thereof or prior to the subsequent purchase. (b)
perturbation.justification: The modification weakens the restriction on the Company's use of the trademark, increasing the risk of trademark infringement. The original text states the use 'is restricted to' and 'shall not use' for other purposes. The changed text introduces ambiguity by using 'should primarily be for' and 'will attempt not to use, as much as possible,' making the obligation non-binding. While the scraped law snippet 1 does not directly address the weakening of restrictions, it defines trademark infringement, supporting the argument that weakening restrictions can lead to infringement. The changed text contradicts the intent of trademark law, which aims to protect trademarks from unauthorized use.

perturbation.original_text: At least three business days prior to the dissemination of any such public announcement or filing containing the above-required description, the Company shall submit to AGI for its review and comment the proposed public announcement or description.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will endeavor to submit to AGI, when feasible, a draft of any public announcement or filing containing the above-required description, some time before dissemination, for its review and possible comment.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'At least three business days prior' to 'some time before dissemination' and 'shall submit' to 'will endeavor to submit, when feasible', the obligation to provide timely notification and mandatory submission is weakened. The phrase 'possible comment' also makes AGI's review process non-compulsory.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Corporations Code Section 25403 pertains to false or misleading statements in connection with the offer, sale, or purchase of a security. While this section doesn't directly mandate pre-dissemination review, it imposes liability for misleading public statements. Delaying or omitting AGI's review and comment process (which the original clause intends to facilitate accurate disclosures about their endorsement) could increase the risk of disseminating information that is deemed misleading, which would run contrary to minimizing the risk of violating this section of the Corporations Code.

SECURITIES [25000 - 31528] ( Title 4 added by Stats. 1949, Ch. 384. ) DIVISION 1. CORPORATE SECURITIES LAW OF 1968 [25000 - 25707] ( Division 1 repealed and added by Stats. 1968, Ch. 88. ) PART 5. FRAUDULENT AND PROHIBITED PRACTICES [25400 - 25404] ( Part 5 added by Stats. 1968, Ch. 88. ) 25403. (a) Every person who with knowledge directly or indirectly controls and induces any person to violate any provision of this division or any rule or order thereunder shall be deemed to be in violation of that provision, rule, or order to the same extent as the controlled and induced person. (b) Any person that knowingly provides substantial assistance to another person in violation of any provision of this division or any rule or order thereunder shall be deemed to be in violation of that provision, rule, or order to the same extent as the person to whom the assistance was provided. (c) It shall be unlawful for any person directly or indirectly to do any act or thing which would be unlawful for that person to do under any provision of this division or any rule or order thereunder through or by any other person. (d) Nothing in this section shall be construed to limit the power of the state to punish any person for any conduct which constitutes a crime und
perturbation.justification: The modification weakens the original obligation, potentially leading to misleading public statements, which could violate California Corporations Code Section 25403. The original text mandated submission 'at least three business days prior' and 'shall submit'. The changed text uses 'will endeavor to submit, when feasible, a draft...some time before dissemination', significantly weakening the obligation. Snippet 1 mentions that 'Every person who with knowledge directly or indirectly controls and induces any person to violate any provision of this division...shall be deemed to be in violation of that provision...' This suggests liability extends to those who facilitate violations. By weakening the review process, the company increases the risk of disseminating misleading information, which could be seen as inducing a violation. The ambiguity introduced increases this risk, thus contradicting the intent to prevent fraudulent practices.
